summary: Docker build failed during a `wget` download from GitHub releases. The server returned HTTP 502 Bad Gateway, causing the wget command to exit with code 1.
root_cause: wget received HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway error when downloading nydus release tarball from https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/v2.4.0/nydus-static-v2.4.0-linux-amd64.tgz

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Explicit 502 error from wget indicating a transient server/network failure

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Build process terminated because wget command failed during network download

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Docker buildx bake reported failure caused by the wget wget command returning non-zero exit code

suggested_fix: Retry the CI job. The 502 error is transient (temporary server issue). If this persists, consider adding a retry mechanism in the Dockerfile for the wget command.

summary: Functional test failed due to GitHub returning repeated 502 Bad Gateway errors when attempting to download a test package, causing HTTP connection pool to exhaust retries.
root_cause: HTTPSConnectionPool to github.com exhausted retries after receiving multiple 502 error responses while attempting to fetch https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip

evidence.quote: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Direct error message explicitly indicating network failure due to 502 responses from GitHub

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError
evidence.reason: pip install command failed with OSError caused by network connectivity issue
Only 1 of 1148 functional tests failed, indicating isolated transient issue rather than code defect
Test explicitly marked as network test, confirming network connectivity is required and subject to transient failures

suggested_fix: Retry the test; this is a transient GitHub infrastructure issue. If failures persist, verify GitHub status and network connectivity.

summary: Jest test suite failed with 1 failing test out of 485 total tests. The failure occurred in test/config/get.ts at line 32, causing the pnpm recursive test run to exit with status 1.
root_cause: A test assertion in test/config/get.ts failed at line 32, column 38. The test expectation did not match the actual behavior, though the specific assertion details are not visible in the log output.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Confirms one test suite failed out of 65 total, indicating test execution completed but with assertion failure
Shows exactly 1 test failed out of 485, confirming a single test assertion failure
Stack trace directly identifies the failing test location and the specific line/column where the assertion failed
Confirms the test run exited with failure status 1, caused by the failing test suite in the recursive run

suggested_fix: Examine test/config/get.ts at line 32 to identify what assertion failed, then investigate whether the implementation code changed or the test expectations need updating.
